conjecture_title: Optimal disjoint-edges bound in complete simple topological graphs
paper_title: A structure theorem for pseudo-segments and its applications
summary: The best known lower bound for pairwise disjoint edges in a complete $n$-vertex simple topological graph is $\Omega(n^{1/2})$, established by Aichholzer, García, Tejel, Vogtenhuber, and Weinberger (SoCG 2022, arXiv:2203.06143), improving the earlier $\Omega(n^{1/3})$ bound of Suk (2012). The source paper (Fox–Pach–Suk, 2023) provides a nearly-polynomial improvement under a density assumption via a pseudo-segment regularity lemma, but the full $\Omega(n)$ conjecture remains open. No post-2023 paper resolving the problem was found in a broad web search.
search_queries: complete simple topological graph pairwise disjoint edges omega(n) lower bound Fox Pach Suk
simple topological graph disjoint edges 2312.01028 pseudo-segments
complete simple topological graph n pairwise disjoint edges linear lower bound 2024 2025
Aichholzer simple topological graph disjoint edges n^{1/2} OR sqrt(n) 2021 2022 2023
notes: The Omega(n^{1/2}) bound (Aichholzer et al., SoCG 2022, arXiv:2203.06143) is the current state of the art; the source paper improves this to a nearly-polynomial bound only under an edge-density assumption (Corollary 1.3). The paper 2512.04795 (December 2025) addresses plane paths in dense topological graphs but does not resolve the disjoint-edges question. No follow-up resolving the full Omega(n) conjecture was found.
